other_observers: Don't know.; I was the original finder.
description: The bird was a small loon with a long, thin, pointed, blue-gray bill that was upturned. The head was somewhat rounded and was a bit snake-like in shape. The crown and nape were a dark gray-brown. The lores, face, throat, side neck, breast, and belly were white. The upperparts were grayish brown, heavily sprinked on back with small white spots. The flanks had dark brown diffuse streaks. The wings appeared black. I was able to get nice comparisons between it and nearby Common Loons and the Red-throated was noticeably smaller.Behaviors: Observed swimming and diving.Call: None heard.Plumage: Adult basic
